title: Buzzing For Life
text: More than 50 years after Rachel Carson’s illuminating study of pesticides in Silent Spring, we’re still dealing with chemicals and their effects on ecosystems. The latest perpetrator: sulfoxaflor, a bug and plant killer recently reapproved by the Environmental Protection Agency. The latest victim: bees. We’re losing billions of bees each year due to complex factors like decreasing agricultural diversity, habitat loss and climate change. However, one threat to bees remains under our control: pesticides. While pesticides are designed to kill pests and insects that damage crops, they also have unintended consequences like eradicating entire harmless species. These include bees, which are essential for harvests. Besides vegetables and fruit trees, bees fertilize other crops that don’t instantly come to mind when we think of bees —cotton, coffee, tea and chocolate. Moreover, there are whole industries that produce various beeswax and honey-based goods. Together, they are multibillion-dollar industries whose collapse could seriously impact on the economy by putting tens of thousands out of work. Bees alone contribute $15 billion annually to U.S. agriculture, and pollinators in general contribute $24 billion to the country’s economy. Moreover, since the U.S. is the largest food exporter in the world and as the global population keeps rising, bees’ survival is essential not only for the U.S. but also for fighting world hunger. Globally, it’s estimated that pollinators, including bees, affect one-third of the world’s food supply. Bees have evolved into mega-pollinators over millions of years, and plants have adapted to incorporate them into their life cycle. If bees disappeared quickly, plants wouldn’t have time to adjust. Many plant species would simply die off, including not only crops but also flowers and trees, which also need pollinators.

questions.prompt: According to the text, the use of pesticides...

options.A: is a direct consequence of climate change

questions.prompt: According to the text, the decline in bee population...

options.A: is now being reported by farmers
options.B: may reduce food stocks all over the world
options.C: is entirely due to the use of pesticides

questions.prompt: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency has announced an upcoming ban on the use of sulfoxaflor.

justification.text: “The latest perpetrator: sulfoxaflor, a bug and plant killer recently reapproved by the Environmental Protection Agency.”
validTexts: The latest perpetrator: sulfoxaflor, a bug and plant killer recently reapproved by the Environmental Protection Agency.
